meta.title: cede / ceed / cess

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: Three spellings, one root: a deliberate early lesson that a root's letters can shift while its meaning holds. recession held out as the least obviously movement-related member. Owner review pending.

unit.label: cede / ceed / cess
unit.mentor_note: Movement is the oldest idea hiding in this root. Once you see it, words that felt unrelated start pointing the same way.

members.meaning: to go ahead, to be successful
to go before, to go forward
to go ahead of, to go beyond

discovery.understand_note: Cede, ceed, and cess are three spellings of one root: to go, or movement. Precede goes before. Proceed goes forward. Exceed goes beyond a limit. Recede goes back. The spelling shifts with the word around it; the movement never does.

applies.prompt: Knowing cede/cess means to go or move, what do you think a recession is?

options.text: A period when economic activity is moving backward, or declining
A formal ceremony where territory is handed over
A sudden and complete stop in all activity
